articles.title: Markets Steady as War Fatigue Sets In, Focus Shifts to Fed and Global Central Banks
articles.expand: Markets were steady in Asian session today, with equities posting modest gains led by Japan, as investors showed signs of “war fatigue” amid the ongoing Middle East conflict. Despite continued escalation between Iran, Israel and the U.S., price action across major asset classes has become increasingly muted, suggesting that geopolitical risks are n...

articles.title: Canadian Dollar softens ahead of Fed, BoC rate decisions
articles.expand: The USD/CAD pair trades with mild gains around 1.3700 during the early European trading hours on Wednesday. Markets turn cautious ahead of critical interest rate decisions from both the Federal Reserve (Fed) and the Bank of Canada (BoC) later on Wednesday.

articles.title: Gold extends the range play around $5,000 as Middle East tensions lend support ahead of Fed
articles.expand: Gold (XAU/USD) extends its sideways consolidative price move around the $5,000 psychological mark heading into the European session on Wednesday as traders seem hesitant ahead of the crucial FOMC decision.

articles.expand: The AUD/USD pair trades 0.15% higher at around 0.7115 during the late Asian trading session on Wednesday.

articles.title: USD/INR opens flat as Fed’s policy comes under the spotlight
articles.expand: The Indian Rupee (INR) opens almost flat against the US Dollar (USD) on Wednesday. The USD/INR pair wobbles around 92.80 as investors shift to the sidelines ahead of the Federal Reserve’s (Fed) monetary policy announcement at 23:30 IST (18:00 GMT).

articles.title: “Hawkish hold” may disappoint as Fed avoids committing either way
articles.expand: The Federal Reserve is widely expected to leave interest rates unchanged at 3.50–3.75% today, with little room for surprise on the decision itself. Instead, markets are focused squarely on the message. While many participants are bracing for a “hawkish hold,” that expectation may prove too simplistic for a Fed facing growing risks on both sides [&#...

articles.title: EUR/GBP weakens below 0.8650 as traders await ECB, BoE rate decisions
articles.expand: The EUR/GBP cross loses ground to near 0.8635, snapping the four-day winning streak during the early European session on Wednesday. Markets are in a "wait-and-see" mode ahead of the European Central Bank (ECB) and the Bank of England (BoE) interest rate decisions later on Thursday.

articles.title: USD/CHF holds gains near 0.7850 ahead of Fed interest rate decision
articles.expand: USD/CHF gains ground after two days of losses, trading around 0.7850 during the Asian hours on Wednesday. The pair holds ground as the US Dollar (USD) remains steady on market caution ahead of the Federal Reserve’s (Fed) policy decision.

articles.title: Gold remains depressed below $5,000 as Fed expectations counter Middle East tensions
articles.expand: Gold (XAU/USD) edges lower during the Asian session on Wednesday, though it remains confined in a range held since the beginning of this week as traders opt to wait for the crucial FOMC decision.

articles.title: EUR/USD Price Forecast: Flat lines below 200-hour SMA/1.1550 ahead of central bank events
articles.expand: The EUR/USD pair struggles to capitalize on this week's goodish recovery move from the 1.1415-1.1410 area, or its lowest level since August 2025, and oscillates in a narrow band during the Asian session on Wednesday.

articles.expand: The Japanese Yen (JPY) trades almost flat around 159.00 against the US Dollar (USD) during the Asian trading session on Wednesday. The USD/JPY pair consolidates as investors await the Federal Reserve’s (Fed) monetary policy announcement at 18:00 GMT.

articles.expand: The NZD/USD pair edges higher during the Asian session on Wednesday and currently trades just above mid-0.5800s, though it lacks bullish conviction.

articles.expand: The Australian Dollar extended its gains for the second straight day after the Reserve Bank of Australia raised rates by 25 basis points, as expected. At the time of writing, the AUD/USD trades at 0.7104, up 0.48%.

articles.title: EUR/USD Price Forecast: Winds of war keep blowing as central banks’ decisions loom
articles.expand: The EUR/USD pair hovers around 1.1530 on Tuesday, reversing an early slump to 1.1466. Financial markets are between a rock and a hard place, as the ongoing escalation of the Iran war and mounting concerns about oil supply disruptions are fueling worldwide inflation.

articles.title: SNB expected to keep key policy rate at 0% through the year - poll
articles.expand: Of note, all but one of 29 economists from major houses expect the SNB to keep its policy rate unchanged throughout the year. The lone exception is Barclays, who forecasts that the Swiss central bank is to cut interest rates to -0.25% in Q2 2026 and hold that until year-end.
